channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Driverless 'bus of the future' is tested in Barcelona
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — Commuters in downtown Barcelona have been able to ride the bus for free this week. There’s just one catch: this mini-bus has no one at the wheel.
item.dc:creator: HERNAN MUÑOZ and JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SpaceX launches a new crew to the space station to replace NASA's stuck astronauts
item.description: The replacements for NASA's two stuck astronauts are on their way to the International Space Station.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Human firewalls: The first line of defense against cyber threats in 2025
item.description: Now that all the industry thought leaders have weighed in and published their predictions for what the cybersecurity landscape will look like in 2025, it seems that there’s a clear consensus emerging: Both attacks and defense of devices and networks will increasingly be AI-driven as time goes by.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Apple Original Films will take you behind-the-scenes of a racing cockpit in this new thrilling F1 movie trailer
item.description: The trailer for the new Apple TV+ Original movie F1 shows off Brad Pitt and Damson Idris in a racing comeback story.
item.dc:creator: amelia.schwanke@futurenet.com (Amelia Schwanke), TechRadar

item.title: Siri’s delay is an artifact of Apple’s unsure AI footing—and more
item.description: Before I go any further, a programming note: On Thursday, March 27 at 1 p.m. ET, my colleague Max Ufberg and I will host “The AI Tools We Love Right Now—and What’s Next,” an online event exclusively for Fast Company Premium subscribers.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Bay Area adds jobs in January while California hiring grinds to a halt
item.description: The Bay Area gained jobs in January, fueled by sturdy gains in the South Bay and East Bay, although the San Francisco metro region lost jobs.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Oracle could still end up running TikTok
item.description: TikTok is just weeks away from shutting operations in the US, unless it reaches a deal - and Oracle could be in pole position.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Postponed NASA, Space X Falcon 9 mission rescheduled for Friday
item.description: NASA and SpaceX rescheduled the launch of their Falcon 9 rocket rescue mission to the International Space Station for Friday. They were unable to launch on Wednesday due to issues with equipment on the ground, and opted to scrub a Thursday launch attempt due to weather.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: The future of AI isn’t the model—it’s the system
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Trump calls EU ‘nasty’ while sitting alongside NATO secretary
item.description: President Trump on Thursday called the European Union "nasty" while sitting alongside NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte. Trump complained about the steep tariff on U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Greece says ambitious Mediterranean power cable project is on track despite setbacks
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — The future of an ambitious electricity cable linking the power grids of Greece, Cyprus and Israel by the end of the decade is on track despite a series of setbacks, Greece’s Foreign Minister Giorgos Gerapetritis said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Intel shares jump after naming chip industry veteran Tan CEO
item.description: Shares of Intel Corp. jumped the most in five years after the company named Lip-Bu Tan as its next chief executive officer, entrusting a former board member and semiconductor veteran with one of the toughest jobs in the chip industry.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: AI-Powered Hotel Set to Open in Las Vegas Area
item.description: The first AI-powered hotel, Otonomous Hotel, is set to open in the Las Vegas area on July 1. The hotel uses machine learning to create a personalized experience for guests.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Tisminezky, NewsNation

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Asian elephant population in Cambodia is more robust than previously thought, a study finds
item.description: A genetic study of Asian elephants in northern Cambodia reveals a larger and more robust population than previously thought, raising hopes the endangered species could slowly recover.
item.dc:creator: Sopheng Cheang And David Rising, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft faces FTC antitrust probe over AI and licensing practices
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) is reportedly pressing ahead with an antitrust investigation into Microsoft, a move that could reshape competition in AI and productivity software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Gov tech layoffs could hamper SHARE IT Act software mandates
item.description: The SHARE IT Act requires agencies to share custom-developed software code. But the termination of 18F and other tech experts could hamper the law's goals. The post Gov tech layoffs could hamper SHARE IT Act software mandates first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Google reportedly looking at smart glasses
item.description: After abandoning its Google Glass smart glasses in 2023, Google seems ready to take another kick at the can with what will reportedly be a $115 million acquisition of Canadian startup AdHawk Microsystems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Preparing for the impact of AI in government contracting projects
item.description: The impact of artificial intelligence in government contracting projects is transformative as it creates new avenues to be better and stand out. The post Preparing for the impact of AI in government contracting projects first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: White House nominates top leaders for CISA, other DHS components
item.description: Sean Plankey has been tapped to lead CISA after serving in key cyber roles during Trump's first term. His nomination comes as CISA grapples with recent cuts. The post White House nominates top leaders for CISA, other DHS components first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: The Copyright Office takes on the sticky issue of artificial intelligence
item.description: The U.S. Copyright Office completed the second of two studies of AI and copyrights as it dealt with whether you can copyright outputs created using AI. The post The Copyright Office takes on the sticky issue of artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dangerous malware targets you through fake loan app
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says a malware app called SpyLend is posing as a financial tool, hitting 100,000-plus downloads.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Weaviate adds agents to its tech stack to ease gen AI app development
item.description: Vector database provider Weaviate has added three new agents to its development stack to facilitate the development of generative AI-based applications. These agents are modular agentic workflows that use large language models (LLMs) and prompts to interact dynamically with data in Weaviate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11
